description:
Implement dual-tailnet support on work macOS laptop(s) so a personal tailnet and a
work tailnet/Headscale can be connected simultaneously, following the macOS recipe in
tailscale/tailscale#16430 (comment 4153566084).

## Current state (as installed)

- Host `ash` (aarch64-darwin), features: work, firefly, tailscale, laptop, mac.
- Managed solely by `darwin-modules/features/tailscale.nix` -> `homebrew.casks = [ "tailscale-app" ]`,
  gated on `machine.features.tailscale` (`features/default.nix:18`).
- `tailscale-app` = standalone GUI app (`/Applications/Tailscale.app`, v1.98.8): macOS
  system/network extension owning a real kernel TUN (utun) on default WG port 41641.
  CLI shim at `/usr/local/bin/tailscale`.
- Currently logged into the PERSONAL tailnet (yann.hodique@, van-scylla.ts.net) — the same
  tailnet the repo depends on: `home-modules/proxy-urls.nix` (ai.van-scylla.ts.net) and
  `modules/nebula/helpers.nix` shared-domain host resolution.
- No open-source tailscaled / Homebrew tailscale formula present.

## Approach (from issue #16430)

Keep the GUI app as PRIMARY (real TUN). Run a SECOND open-source tailscaled in
`--tun=userspace-networking` mode with its own `--socket`, `--statedir`, `--port=41642`,
logged into the work tailnet/Headscale. No TUN conflict; socket/state/port fully isolate
the two daemons. Persist via launchd.

## Feasibility: FEASIBLE — implement the nix-idiomatic version, not the raw recipe

1. Use nixpkgs `tailscale` for the daemon, NOT Homebrew. Confirmed `nixpkgs#tailscale`
   supports aarch64-darwin (builds v1.94.2, drv evaluates). Userspace-networking needs NO
   TCC permission and NO network extension (just a userspace UDP socket), so the
   feedback_macos_tcc_stable_path rule (TCC-gated tools via Homebrew) does NOT apply here.
2. Persist via nix-darwin `launchd.daemons.<name>` (pattern already used in
   `darwin-modules/apps/kanata.nix:205`), not a hand-written /Library/LaunchDaemons plist.
3. Model as a feature module: extend `features/tailscale.nix` or add
   `features/tailscale-secondary.nix`, gated on a new option, so only work laptops get it.
   statedir `/var/lib/tailscale-<name>`, socket `/var/run/tailscale-<name>.sock`, port 41642.

## KEY CAVEAT — access model (the recipe glosses over this)

`--tun=userspace-networking` creates NO network interface, so the OS has no route to the
second tailnet's 100.x addresses. You CANNOT `ssh work-host` or use MagicDNS directly.
Traffic must go through the proxy tailscaled exposes (recipe omits this):
- add `--socks5-server=localhost:1055` and/or `--outbound-http-proxy-listen=localhost:1055`
- point tools at it: ALL_PROXY=socks5://localhost:1055, ssh ProxyCommand, curl --proxy,
  browser proxy for the work domain
- names resolve only via socks5h remote DNS through the proxy, or use raw 100.x IPs
Good for "reach a few work services / Headscale hosts from CLI tools"; NOT a transparent
full-VPN replacement (no ping, no subnet routes into kernel, no printers).

## Gotchas

- Port must stay 41642 (!= 41641 GUI).
- macOS app firewall may prompt to allow the new binary; if blocked, falls back to DERP
  relay (works, slower).
- PATH `tailscale` (/usr/local/bin) talks to the GUI socket; work daemon needs
  `tailscale --socket=...` — wrap as `tailscale-work`.
- Login is a one-time imperative `sudo tailscale --socket=... login [--login-server=...]`;
  an auth key via sops-nix could automate re-auth.
- Version skew (1.94 nix vs 1.98 app) is fine — daemons are independent.
- Keep gated off non-work machines (spectre has tailscale but not work).

## DECISION REQUIRED before implementation

The transparent slot (full routing + MagicDNS + subnet routes) can only be the GUI app;
the other tailnet becomes proxy-only userspace.
- Work needs full VPN behavior -> make WORK the GUI primary, personal the userspace
  secondary, and re-point the repo's van-scylla.ts.net proxy usage.
- Work is just Headscale + a few services -> keep PERSONAL as GUI primary, add work as
  userspace secondary (lowest friction; nothing else in the repo changes).
Also needed: is the work side a Tailscale org or Headscale (changes --login-server)?

## Deliverable

Feature module (nixpkgs tailscale + launchd.daemons + SOCKS5 proxy + `tailscale-work`
wrapper), gated behind a new option for work laptops (ash), plus documented one-time
login bootstrap step.
title: Dual-tailnet support on work macOS laptop (userspace tailscaled)
